Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Complex A highly skilled and well-resourced threat actor group, C0010, conducts a multi-stage cyber attack targeting critical infrastructure in the water and wastewater sector. Their objective is to disrupt operations and exfiltrate sensitive data. They employ sophisticated techniques to infiltrate, maintain persistence, escalate privileges, evade defenses, and ultimately impact the targeted systems.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Network Traffic, Script File, Target Artifact, Application Log, Logon Session, Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Network Traffic Flow, Response Metadata, Web Shell, Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism, Hide Artifacts, Mimikatz Tool, System Network Configuration Discovery, Application Log Content, Logon Session Creation, Data Interchange Protocol Manipulation, Exfiltration Over C2 Channel, Process Creation

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309; Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: The attackers conduct extensive network scans to identify vulnerabilities in the water and wastewater systems' network architecture.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522; Name: Malicious Hardware Component Replacement
stage.description: The attackers establish command and control servers and acquire infrastructure necessary for malware deployment and communication channels.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2017-6334 (Suggested): dnslookup.cgi on NETGEAR DGN2200 devices with firmware through 10.0.0.50 allows remote authenticated users to execute arbitrary OS commands
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 439; Name: Manipulation During Distribution
stage.description: The attackers infiltrate the OT environment by compromising software updates, leading to unauthorized execution of commands on internet-facing assets.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2023-6345 (Suggested): Google Chromium Skia contains an integer overflow vulnerability that allows a remote attacker, who has compromised the renderer process, to potentially perform a sandbox escape via a malicious file.
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 121; Name: Exploit Non-Production Interfaces
stage.description: Exploiting known vulnerabilities in web interfaces, the attackers gain initial access and execute malicious payloads.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 650; Name: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: To maintain persistent access, the attackers deploy web shells on vulnerable script files within the system.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers escalate their privileges within the OT environment to gain higher-level access and control over critical processes.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 498; Name: Probe iOS Screenshots
stage.description: To avoid detection, the attackers hide artifacts associated with their malicious activities, making it difficult for security tools to detect their presence.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 226; Name: Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation
stage.description: The attackers steal credentials using tools like Mimikatz to facilitate lateral movement throughout the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309; Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: Understanding the network layout, the attackers plan their lateral movement to maximize impact.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Using stolen credentials, the attackers move laterally across the network to reach critical systems.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 277; Name: Data Interchange Protocol Manipulation
stage.description: The attackers gather sensitive data related to system configurations and operations.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 24; Name: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: Data collected is transferred back to the attackers' controlled infrastructure via established C2 channels.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers disrupt services by destroying backup data and corrupting systems, aiming to inhibit recovery efforts.

Scenario.summary: The complex cyber attack orchestrated by the expert threat actor group C0010 against the water and wastewater systems involves multiple stages of reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact. The attackers meticulously plan and execute each phase to achieve maximum disruption and data theft, demonstrating a high level of sophistication and determination.
